mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative when both HOUST and RSAFS show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% signals economic weakness that reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle recessions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive when GDPC1 declines below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong labor market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative when PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 2% signals monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%.
